title: Jason X
review: As a parody of the rest of the series, and also {Alien}, it's a bold idea. In practice it falls woefully short of the mark, looking like a low budget sci-fi TV series of the time, and containing no suspense outside of the five seconds before each kill. Once Jason is awake, you could randomise the order of scenes in this movie and it would make the same amount of sense and be equally entertaining. Each scene has no effect on another, as it's just a series of encounters with Jason in grey corridors where he kills everyone else involved. Most of the movie is played for laughs, but I find the more self-serious movies in the franchise funnier. I wouldn't be surprised if you told me Uwe Boll made this.
spoilers: Even the part when the woman who gets sucked through a metal grate as the spaceship depressurises wasn't nearly as entertaining as I hoped it would be, once it became obvious it was about to happen. Slamming the virtual campers in their sleeping bags like socks full of grapes was the only part that got a real laugh out of me.
